title: Course Schedule
description: There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1. You are given an array prerequisites where prerequisites[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that you must take course bi first if you want to take course ai. Return true if you can finish all courses. Otherwise, return false.
hints: Model the courses as a directed graph where each course is a node and each prerequisite is a directed edge.
The problem reduces to detecting whether the graph contains a cycle.
Use Kahn's algorithm (BFS-based topological sort) or DFS-based cycle detection.
timeComplexity: O(V + E) where V is numCourses and E is the number of prerequisites
spaceComplexity: O(V + E) for the adjacency list and in-degree array

solutions.python:
from collections import deque

def canFinish(numCourses, prerequisites):
    adj = [[] for _ in range(numCourses)]
    in_degree = [0] * numCourses
    for dest, src in prerequisites:
        adj[src].append(dest)
        in_degree[dest] += 1
    queue = deque([i for i in range(numCourses) if in_degree[i] == 0])
    count = 0
    while queue:
        node = queue.popleft()
        count += 1
        for neighbor in adj[node]:
            in_degree[neighbor] -= 1
            if in_degree[neighbor] == 0:
                queue.append(neighbor)
    return count == numCourses

title: Course Schedule II
description: There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1. You are given an array prerequisites where prerequisites[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that you must take course bi first if you want to take course ai. Return the ordering of courses you should take to finish all courses. If there are many valid answers, return any of them. If it is impossible to finish all courses, return an empty array.
hints: This is a classic topological sort problem.
Use Kahn's algorithm: compute in-degrees, start with nodes of in-degree 0, and process them in order.
If the resulting order includes all courses, return it; otherwise return an empty array.

solutions.python:
from collections import deque

def findOrder(numCourses, prerequisites):
    adj = [[] for _ in range(numCourses)]
    in_degree = [0] * numCourses
    for dest, src in prerequisites:
        adj[src].append(dest)
        in_degree[dest] += 1
    queue = deque([i for i in range(numCourses) if in_degree[i] == 0])
    order = []
    while queue:
        node = queue.popleft()
        order.append(node)
        for neighbor in adj[node]:
            in_degree[neighbor] -= 1
            if in_degree[neighbor] == 0:
                queue.append(neighbor)
    return order if len(order) == numCourses else []

title: Alien Dictionary
description: There is a new alien language that uses the Latin alphabet, but the order among the letters are unknown to you. You are given a list of strings words from the alien language's dictionary, where the strings in words are sorted lexicographically according to the language's rules. Now, due to the authorities, you are given a string word. Return a string of the unique letters in the new alien language, sorted in lexicographically ascending order according to the language's rules. If there is no solution, return an empty string.
hints: Compare adjacent words to find ordering constraints between characters.
Build a directed graph where an edge from char a to char b means a comes before b.
Perform topological sort on the graph. If a cycle is detected, return empty string.
timeComplexity: O(C) where C is the total length of all words combined
spaceComplexity: O(1) since alphabet size is fixed at 26, or O(C) for the graph

title: Minimum Height Trees
description: A tree is an undirected graph in which any two nodes are connected by exactly one path. Given a tree of n nodes labelled from 0 to n - 1, and an array of n - 1 edges, and a label n. Return all the labels of the root nodes of minimum height trees. A node's height is the number of edges on the longest path from the node to a leaf.
hints: The root of a minimum height tree must be near the center of the tree.
Repeatedly remove leaf nodes (nodes with degree 1) layer by layer until 1 or 2 nodes remain.
These remaining nodes are the roots of minimum height trees.

title: Find Eventual Safe States
description: There is a directed graph of n nodes with each node labeled from 0 to n - 1. The graph is represented by a 0-indexed 2D integer array graph where graph[i] is an array of nodes that node i has a directed edge to. A node is a terminal node if there are no outgoing edges. A node is a safe node if every possible path starting from that node leads to a terminal node. Return a list containing all the safe nodes of the graph in sorted order.
hints: Safe nodes are those that are not part of any cycle and do not lead to a cycle.
This is equivalent to finding nodes that are not part of or do not reach a cycle.
Use reverse topological sort: start from terminal nodes and work backwards, or use DFS with state tracking.

title: Sequence Reconstruction
description: You are given an array of integers nums that represents a permutation of integers. You are also given a 2D integer array sequences where sequences[i] is a subsequence of nums. Return true if nums is the only supersequence of sequences. In other words, if nums can be uniquely reconstructed from all the subsequences in sequences, return true. Otherwise, return false.
hints: Build a directed graph from the subsequences where an edge from a to b means a must come before b.
Perform topological sort and check that at every step there is exactly one node with in-degree 0.
Also verify that the resulting topological order matches the given nums array.
timeComplexity: O(N + S) where N is the length of nums and S is the total length of all sequences

title: Course Schedule IV
description: There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1. You are given an array prerequisites where prerequisites[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that you must take course bi first if you want to take course ai. After completing a course, you receive a certificate. You are also given some queries where queries[i] = [ui, vi] determines whether you should take course ui before course vi. Return a boolean array answer, where answer[i] is true if you should take course ui before course vi, otherwise false.
hints: Use Floyd-Warshall or BFS/DFS to compute all pairs reachability.
Alternatively, compute topological order and check if u comes before v and there is a path from u to v.
Precompute reachability for all pairs, then answer each query in O(1).
timeComplexity: O(V^3) for Floyd-Warshall or O(V*(V+E)) for BFS from each node

title: Find All Possible Recipes
description: You are given an array of strings recipes where recipes[i] is the name of a recipe. You are also given two string arrays ingredients and supplies. Each recipe has a list of ingredients that must be available in supplies. Return a list of all recipes that can be prepared. A recipe can be prepared if all of its ingredients are available in supplies and can themselves be prepared (recursively). Return the result in any order.

examples.input: recipes = [bread], ingredients = [[yeast, flour]], supplies = [yeast, flour, sugar]
recipes = [bread, sandwich], ingredients = [[yeast, flour], [bread, meat]], supplies = [yeast, flour, meat]

hints: Build a dependency graph where each recipe depends on its ingredients.
Use topological sort: start with ingredients available in supplies, then process recipes whose ingredients are all available.
A recipe can be made once all its prerequisites (ingredients) are satisfied.
timeComplexity: O(R * I) where R is the number of recipes and I is the average number of ingredients
spaceComplexity: O(R * I) for the graph and in-degree tracking

title: Parallel Courses III
description: You are given an integer n, which indicates that there are n courses labeled from 1 to n. You are also given a 2D integer array relations where relations[j] = [prevCoursej, nextCoursej] denotes that course prevCoursej has to be completed before course nextCoursej. You are given a 0-indexed integer array time where time[i] denotes the number of months it takes to complete the (i+1)th course. You must complete all the courses. You can start taking courses at any time if the prerequisites are satisfied. Return the minimum number of months needed to complete all the courses.
hints: This is a topological sort problem where you need to find the longest path in a DAG.
For each course, compute the earliest completion time considering all prerequisite paths.
Use topological sort and relax edges: for each course, its earliest finish time is max of all predecessors' finish times plus its own duration.

title: Longest Increasing Path in a Matrix
description: Given an m x n integers matrix, return the length of the longest increasing path in matrix. From each cell, you can either move in four directions: left, right, up, or down. You may not move diagonally or move outside the boundary. An increasing path is a path where the values are strictly increasing.
hints: Use memoized DFS to avoid recomputing the longest path from each cell.
Alternatively, model the matrix as a DAG where edges go from smaller to larger values and perform topological sort.
For each cell, the longest path is 1 + max of longest paths from all neighbors with larger values.
timeComplexity: O(m * n) since each cell is computed once with memoization
spaceComplexity: O(m * n) for the memoization table
